objective:
I am currently a PhD Candidate in environmental engineering at the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill. My research focuses on molecular-scale simulations of mass transport in membrane materials used for desalination and water reuse.

Generally, my research interests involve multiscale computational physics, spanning from the continuum scale down to the atomistic. I'm interested in developing coarse-grain transformations for atomistic simulations of polymeric materials, which will bring insights generated at the molecular-scale to useful continuum scale models.
